coreCompetencies: Understanding of fairness, transparency and accountability principles in AI
Familiarity with the risk categories of the EU AI Act
Collection of model and dataset documentation under supervision
Assisted computation of fairness metrics such as demographic parity
Reading and interpreting model cards and dataset datasheets
Preparation of registry entries for an AI system inventory
Documentation of ethical issues found during reviews
complementaryCompetencies: Notions of statistics applied to bias measurement
Familiarity with spreadsheets and structured documentation tools
Basic knowledge of the General Data Protection Regulation
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Completes guided documentation and bias-measurement tasks
Needs 3-6 months to become productive

coreCompetencies: Measurement of bias with group metrics such as equal opportunity and equalized odds
Drafting of model cards and datasheets following established templates
Application of explainability tools such as SHAP and LIME on existing models
Preparation of preliminary algorithmic impact assessments
Verification of compliance against regulatory checklists
Analysis of training data representativeness across subgroups
Documentation of the decision traceability of an AI system
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework
Familiarity with fairness libraries such as Fairlearn or AIF360
Understanding of EU AI Act roles: provider and deployer
indicators: Develops bias assessments and documentation with occasional supervision
Resolves medium-complexity questions in model audits
Actively participates in system and documentation reviews

coreCompetencies: Design of end-to-end bias audits on production models
Selection of fairness metrics appropriate to the context and protected groups
Application of bias mitigation techniques before, during and after training
Preparation of algorithmic impact assessments compliant with the EU AI Act
Analysis of global and local explainability to justify model decisions
Definition of acceptance thresholds and blocking criteria for a system
Complete documentation of the model lifecycle for regulatory traceability
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of privacy techniques such as anonymization and differential privacy
Familiarity with evaluating language models for toxicity and bias
Mentoring of entry-level colleagues
indicators: Completes complex fairness audits with minimal supervision
Identifies ethical risks and proposes concrete mitigations
Actively contributes to the design of governance processes

coreCompetencies: Design of model governance frameworks covering the full lifecycle
Definition of the processes of an AI ethics review board
Implementation of EU AI Act compliance for high-risk systems
Design of continuous fairness and model drift monitoring channels
Integration of governance gates into the model development lifecycle
Definition of model and dataset documentation standards
Coordination of bias audits across data science and product teams
Translation of regulatory requirements into verifiable technical controls
complementaryCompetencies: Experience applying the NIST AI Risk Management Framework
Knowledge of data governance and dataset lineage
Familiarity with third-party and vendor system audits
indicators: Leads the design of critical governance processes
Mentors junior specialists and reviews their audits
Makes informed decisions on controls and mitigations

coreCompetencies: Design of an AI governance program for an entire business unit
Definition of an AI risk management framework aligned with the NIST standard
Establishment of a review board with approval and escalation criteria
Definition of fairness and transparency metrics as organizational standards
Design of independent audits and external review processes
Governance of the data lifecycle compliant with GDPR and data minimization
Definition of the response to ethical incidents and algorithmic harms
Integration of impact assessment into deployment decisions
complementaryCompetencies: Comparative knowledge of international AI regulatory frameworks
Design of responsible AI training for technical and product teams
Definition of transparency agreements with affected users
indicators: Designs the AI governance framework of complete products
Defines the team's fairness and transparency standards
Leads governance initiatives spanning multiple teams

coreCompetencies: Direction of the AI governance program serving multiple teams
Definition of accountability frameworks for responsible AI deployment
Technical leadership in continuous fairness and compliance monitoring
Design of the EU AI Act conformity strategy across the portfolio
Governance of third-party model risk and the AI supply chain
Direction of relationships with regulators and external auditors
Design of the traceability and evidence infrastructure for inspections
Establishment of the company's transparency and explainability policy
complementaryCompetencies: Evaluation of governance and model-auditing tools
Knowledge of emerging AI certification standards
Representation of the company in responsible AI forums
indicators: Designs AI governance for multiple teams at once
Influences organizational decisions on AI risk
Is the company's reference in AI ethics and governance

coreCompetencies: Definition of the direction of AI governance across the organization
Design of governance frameworks adaptable to different regulatory regimes
Development of multi-year compliance and responsible AI roadmaps
Strategies to scale governance to new products and jurisdictions
Definition of the cost and return of AI risk controls
Direction of the balance between model innovation and regulatory obligations
Building and growing AI ethics and governance teams
Anticipation of the impact of new regulation on the AI system portfolio
complementaryCompetencies: Participation in public consultations on AI regulation
Publication of responsible AI guidelines or standards
Knowledge of trends in algorithmic auditing and certification
indicators: Defines the organization's AI governance strategy
Is recognized as a reference in AI ethics and governance
Speaks at industry conferences

coreCompetencies: Strategic vision for the evolution of AI governance and regulation
Definition of the role of responsible AI in the company's strategy
Ethical technical due diligence of products, models and acquisitions
Design of the governance framework that supports multiple business lines
Direction of the corporate response to emerging AI regulation
Definition of responsible AI principles against social harms and discrimination
Building and leading AI ethics and governance organizations
Analysis of the business and reputational impact of governance decisions
complementaryCompetencies: Influence on public policy and AI regulation
Advisory to leadership on ethical and regulatory AI risk

indicators: Influences the direction of AI governance in the industry

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership of the company's responsible AI strategy
Alignment of AI governance with business and risk strategy
Direction of investment in governance, compliance and specialized talent

Management of the balance between innovation, public trust and legal obligations
